projectDescription: An Android application that runs Windows (x86_64) applications and games on ARM-based Android devices using Wine, Box86/Box64, and GPU translation layers like DXVK and Turnip.
keyFeatures: Run Windows (x86_64) applications and games on Android ARM devices
Wine integration for Windows application compatibility
Box86/Box64 x86-to-ARM translation with configurable presets (Stability, Compatibility, Performance)
Multiple graphics driver support: Turnip (Vulkan), VirGL, Zink
DirectX translation via DXVK, VKD3D, WineD3D, and CNC DDraw
Container-based isolation for each Windows environment
Custom input controls editor with on-screen gamepad overlay
External controller support with button binding configuration
Per-shortcut settings for individual games and applications
ALSA audio support with configurable driver
Virtual drive mapping to Android storage locations
publicApiSurface: Container — manages Windows environment configuration (screen size, graphics driver, Wine components, drives)
ContainerManager — creates, lists, imports/exports, and deletes containers
Shortcut — stores per-game launch settings (exec path, arguments, container ID)
Box86_64Preset — preset profiles: STABILITY, COMPATIBILITY, INTERMEDIATE, PERFORMANCE, CUSTOM
WineInfo — represents a Wine version (version string, arch, path)
XEnvironment — runtime environment managing components (XServer, ALSA, GuestProgramLauncher, VirGL)
ImageFs — the root filesystem image that hosts Wine and the Ubuntu rootfs
InputControlsManager — manages on-screen control profiles (.icp files)
ControlsProfile — a named set of input control bindings
ExternalController — maps physical gamepad buttons to Windows input events
WineUtils — utility methods for creating dosdevice symlinks, installing Wine builds, running Wine commands
